origin.name: Montenegro
origin.key_document: Izvod iz knjige umrlih (death certificate, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Montenegro travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Podgorica guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Montenegro is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option. You will need the death certificate, cremation certificate, and export documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Izvod iz knjige umrlih (death certificate, certified English translation required)
special_rules: Montenegro is a Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death must be registered with the Maticna sluzba (Civil Registry) promptly.
All Montenegrin-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Montenegrin documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Podgorica can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Montenegro to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Montenegro. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Civil Registry, apostille, and documentation all handled. Contact us 24/7.
